steps.title: Identify the subject of the blank
steps.content: The relative pronoun “which” is the subject of the blank's verb. “Which” refers back to “Kansas City International Airport” — a singular noun.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: A verb must agree in number with its subject. “Is” is the only singular form among the choices; “are,” “were,” and “have been” are all plural and therefore violate agreement. That matches choice A — A is correct.
